key_points: North and South campus split confuses new students navigating dorms
International students worry about housing costs in New York State
On-campus housing exists but off-campus is common for upperclassmen
key_quotes: "I live near south campus too... it's weird seeing south campus" - current student
Just scared about the housing cost as NY is expensive
UB is way too isolated (looks like a ghost town)

key_points: Campus feels isolated and ghost-town-like, especially for city students
Large school size makes finding your crowd harder than expected
International students find social integration challenging but manageable
key_quotes: UB is way too isolated (looks like a ghost town)
People there aren't as friendly as I thought they would be
I'm scared yet happy, I'm an international student so moving is not easy

key_points: C3 dining hall recently renovated and well-regarded by students
Multiple dining halls across split campuses including Ellicott
Students generally satisfied with on-campus food options
key_quotes: I like restaurants on campus and off campus! I'm so proud of our C3 dining hall getting newer and enticing
I used to study here as one of the undergraduates... Wish it's getting better
My dining hall Ellicott Dining hall features in this video

key_points: One student left UB specifically due to depression and academic struggles
Buffalo's cold, grey winters hit students harder than expected
Safety concerns after a campus shooting caused genuine anxiety
key_quotes: I first went to university at buffalo but chose to leave because i was depressed and wasnt doing well in school
After the recent news about the shooting, i've been really anxious about UB... I don't even feel excited about college anymore
The weather here is like the ice planet Hoth

key_points: State school pricing is UB's biggest selling point over private peers
Merit scholarships exist including Presidential Scholarship (full ride)
Financial aid portal is confusing — requires enrollment to view package
key_quotes: I picked Buffalo because 64k in loans is not worth it, I plan to go to graduate school
I got zero financial aid and I can't afford to go to the school at sticker price
For ubuff i got accepted and it says I have to enroll before activating my account — can i not see my financial aid unless i enroll?

key_points: Engineering and CS programs are solid but have brutal weed-out courses
CS program admission is competitive; admitted as 'CS Intended' is common
Curriculum load is heavy — 4 required subjects per semester for some grad programs
key_quotes: It's really easy to be booted out of the Computer Science program, and indeed the entire Engineering program in general
I got an admit for fall 2025 Ms.Cs Program. Just worried about the difficulty of curriculum because it is compulsory to take 4 subjects per semester
I admitted as 'CS Intended,' so the major isn't 100% secured yet

key_points: Accidental withdrawal was processed without human verification despite paid deposit
Financial aid and portal navigation is opaque and frustrating for new students
Rolling admissions process is praised for speed and transparency
key_quotes: 'The University at Buffalo has received and processed your request to withdraw your current application' — I paid the deposit and am panicking
All transcripts etc can be uploaded by yourself... You don't have to send scores. Just upload a screenshot
Rolling admissions — get your response within 2-3 weeks max. Sweet!

key_points: Buffalo city itself is described as cold, depressing, and limited by multiple students
North and South campus split is a constant logistical complaint
Proximity to NYC and Toronto is a genuine plus for internships and travel
key_quotes: Friends told me it's cold, depressing and not that enjoyable
UB is way too isolated (looks like a ghost town)
Close proximity to New York City, providing more potential for internships and job opportunities

key_points: Internship access is a weak spot — students often travel to NYC or have limited local options
Industrial Engineering and Mechanical Engineering have strong job fair presence
OPT and job prospects are a major concern for international students post-graduation
key_quotes: I wanted to know if internship is a con at UB as there aren't much companies due to which students have to travel to NY city
Most companies that came [to the job fair] are related to either Industrial Engineering or Mechanical or Civil Engineering
Is the OPT and job potential good after doing this course there?

key_points: Among the best value STEM options in New York State for in-state students
Out-of-state costs can approach private school prices without merit aid
Frequently chosen over RIT, RPI, and even Purdue purely on cost grounds
key_quotes: I picked Buffalo because 64k in loans is not worth it, I plan to go to graduate school, and I liked Buffalo's campus better
For Suny Buffalo I would be going for about $20,000 a year while RPI would be about $40,000
UB most likely will still be the cheapest option

key_points: Academically serious but socially quiet — not a traditional 'college experience' school
Strong international student community, especially Indian and South Asian students
Students feel proud of UB as a launching pad despite its safety-school reputation
key_quotes: Welcome to buffalo. If you do decide to come here you'll have fun trust me
I know it's not that big of a school and nor is it a top choice but I'm just happy
UB seemed more academically focused overall... very transparent and had their professors and students speaking openly

key_points: **Weather/Environment:** Multiple students describe Buffalo as cold, depressing, and isolating
**CS Access:** Admitted as 'CS Intended' with no guaranteed major entry is a real risk
**Administration:** Paid deposit withdrawal processed automatically with no human check
key_quotes: Friends told me it's cold, depressing and not that enjoyable
I am admitted as 'CS Intended,' so the major isn't 100% secured yet
I have paid the deposit fee and housing fee. After seeing the withdrawal, I am panicking

key_points: Presidential Scholarship offers full ride — interview-based and worth pursuing
Rolling admissions with fast decisions and no required essay is unusually student-friendly
Honors College provides a tighter academic community within a large university
key_quotes: I'm doing an interview for the Presidential Scholarship at UB, if received, I would have a full ride there
No essay required. You can submit one, but it's not necessary. I didn't. Rolling admissions — get your response within 2-3 weeks max
I got accepted into UB's Honors College!
